description: Fix CLI UX friction points discovered during fresh-user experience testing in a Docker container with clean state. Two issues remain unfixed in source: (1) The Claude Code MCP config path shown by `opentabs start` says `~/.claude.json` but Claude Code reads from `~/.claude/settings/mcp.json` — users put their config in the wrong file. (2) The `opentabs config set tool.` message says 'No tools available (no plugins installed)' which confuses users who see 37 tools in `opentabs status` — it should clarify that it only lists plugin tools, not browser tools. Other issues found during testing (missing auth headers on first run, bare `opentabs` showing help, plugin search returning nothing) were already fixed in source but not yet published.

userStories.title: Fix Claude Code MCP config file path in start output
userStories.description: As a new user, I want the Claude Code config path shown by `opentabs start` to match the actual location that Claude Code reads so that I put my config in the right file.
acceptanceCriteria: The Claude Code entry in `printMcpClientConfigs` shows `~/.claude/settings/mcp.json` instead of `~/.claude.json — add to "mcpServers"`
The JSON structure printed matches what Claude Code expects in `~/.claude/settings/mcp.json` (the mcpServers wrapper is correct)
The `doctor` command's MCP client config check and the `start` command's printed config use the same file path for Claude Code (`~/.claude/settings/mcp.json`)
userStories.notes: In `platform/cli/src/commands/start.ts` line 163, the Claude Code config entry has `file: '~/.claude.json — add to "mcpServers"'`. Change this to `file: '~/.claude/settings/mcp.json'`. The doctor command at `platform/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts` line 236 already checks `join(homedir(), '.claude', 'settings', 'mcp.json')` — the start command should match this path. The JSON structure `{ "mcpServers": { "opentabs": { ... } } }` is already correct for this file — only the `file` label string needs to change. This is a single string change in one file.

userStories.title: Clarify 'no tools available' message in config set tool.
userStories.description: As a user, I want the `opentabs config set tool.` message to clarify it only lists plugin tools so that I understand why it says 'no tools' when `opentabs status` shows 37 tools.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `opentabs config set tool.` when no plugins are installed shows a message that mentions 'plugin tools' specifically, not just 'tools'
The message mentions that browser tools are managed separately via `browser-tool.<name>`
userStories.notes: In `platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts` line 182, the message `'No tools available (no plugins installed).'` should be changed to something like `'No plugin tools available (no plugins installed).\nBrowser tools are managed with: opentabs config set browser-tool.<name> enabled|disabled'`. The `handleListTools` function (starting at line 168) is what runs when the user types `opentabs config set tool.` — it fetches plugin tool names from the server and if the list is empty, shows this message. The existing message on line 177 (`'You can also list installed plugins with: opentabs plugin list'`) could also be updated to mention the browser-tool prefix. Keep the change minimal — just update the two console.log lines in the `tools.length === 0` branch.
